classes.description: The agent lacked repo-local context or read the wrong context.
The agent claimed completion without sufficient evidence.
The change violated intended boundaries, dependency direction, or provider contracts.
The task was possible to ship because the relevant behavior had no deterministic check.
Docs, claims, registry, or templates diverged from actual behavior.
The agent used the wrong tool, tool mode, command, or rewrite mechanism.
Tool permissions allowed too much, denied required work, or drifted from skill contracts.
The harness behaved differently in real Claude/Codex runtime than in synthetic tests.
The eval rubric or fixture measured process but missed product truth.
The agent spent excessive tokens, turns, or API cost for the task risk.
The failure is tied to model behavior after the harness supplied adequate context and checks.
